artifacts.description: Structured log of the search queries used in this run and why they did not yield a usable primary dataset for the requested analysis.

Narrative feasibility assessment explaining why the provided datasets and retrieved sources could not support an honest original quantitative study of refractory epilepsy in pediatric Asian populations.

limitations: No provided dataset was suitable for estimating refractory epilepsy prevalence in pediatric Asian populations.
The web search results available in this run surfaced literature summaries and reviews, not a directly retrievable primary dataset for original quantitative estimation.

Without a usable cohort, registry, survey, or case-series dataset containing both pediatric Asian population denominators and refractory-epilepsy status, meaningful original analysis was not possible.
Because the requested study could not be executed faithfully with available materials, I returned no quantitative results rather than overclaiming from secondary literature.
methods: I inspected the user-provided dataset hints and assessed whether any were suitable for an original empirical analysis of refractory epilepsy in pediatric Asian populations. BRFSS 2015 was rejected as unrelated to epilepsy or pediatrics; the NCI Genomic Data Commons API was rejected as oncology-focused; MAST was rejected as astronomy-only; and DANDI was considered potentially relevant only for neuroscience recordings, but no specific retrievable dandiset or metadata structure supporting an estimate of refractory-epilepsy prevalence in Asian pediatric populations was identified in this run. I then searched for open data specifically on pediatric epilepsy, refractory epilepsy, Asia, and prevalence. The returned materials were literature pages and reviews rather than an accessible primary dataset suitable for computing a new prevalence estimate. I saved a reproducible search log and feasibility notes as artifacts.
research_question: Can the prevalence of refractory epilepsy among pediatric populations in Asia be estimated using the datasets and retrievable sources available in this run?

queries: pediatric epilepsy refractory prevalence Asia study open data dataset
epilepsy children Asia refractory open dataset prevalence

sources.label: Search process documented from web queries for pediatric refractory epilepsy in Asia
sources.notes: Returned pages were literature/review-style resources and did not provide a directly usable primary dataset for original empirical estimation in this run.

title: Feasibility assessment for estimating refractory epilepsy burden in pediatric Asian populations
